title: Psalms, Chapter 11
description: Psalms chapter 11. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 90. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 90.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>ειϲ το τελοϲ ϋπερ τηϲ ογδοηϲ ψαλμοϲ τω δαυειδ <sup>2</sup>ϲωϲον με κε οτι εκλελοιπεν οϲιοϲ οτι ωλιγωθηϲαν αι αληθειαι απο > των υιων των ανθρωπων <sup>3</sup>ματαια ελαληϲαν εκαϲτοϲ προϲ τον πληϲιον αυτου χειλη δολια εν καρδια και εν καρδια ελαληϲαν <sup>4</sup>εξολεθρευϲαι κϲ παντα τα χειλη τα δολια και γλωϲϲαν μεγαλορημονα <sup>5</sup>τουϲ ειπονταϲ την γλωϲϲαν ημων μεγαλυνουμεν τα χειλη ημων παρ ημων εϲτιν τιϲ ημων κϲ εϲτιν
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>In the LORD put I my trust: How say ye to my soul, Flee
as
 a bird to your mountain? <sup>2</sup>For, lo, the wicked bend
their bow, they make ready their arrow upon the string, that they may privily shoot at the upright in heart. <sup>3</sup>If the foundations be destroyed, what can the righteous do? <sup>4</sup>The LORD
is in his holy temple, the LORD’S throne
is
 in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men. <sup>5</sup>The LORD trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>απο τηϲ ταλαι>πωριαϲ των πτωχων και απο του ϲτεναγμου των πενητω νυν αναϲτηϲομαι λεγει κϲ θηϲομαι εν ϲωτηρια παρρηϲιαϲομαι εν αυτω <sup>7</sup>τα λογια κυ λογια αγνα αργυριον πεπυρωμενον δοκιμιον τη γη κεκαθαριϲμενον επταπλαϲιωϲ <sup>8</sup>ϲυ κε φυλαξειϲ ημαϲ και διατηρηϲειϲ ημαϲ απο τηϲ γενεαϲ ταυτηϲ και ειϲ τον αιωνα <sup>9</sup>κυκλω οι αϲεβειϲ περιπατουϲιν κατα το ϋψοϲ ϲου επολυωρηϲαϲ τουϲ υιουϲ των ανθρωπων
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>Upon the wicked he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone, and an horrible tempest:
this shall be the portion of their cup. <sup>7</sup>For the righteous LORD loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.
